root_text.sn55.11:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati rājakārāme.
root_text.sn55.11:1.2: Atha kho sahassabhikkhunisaṅgho yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ aṭṭhāsi. Ekamantaṁ ṭhitā kho tā bhikkhuniyo bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.sn55.11:2.1: “Catūhi kho, bhikkhuniyo, dhammehi samannāgato ariyasāvako sotāpanno hoti avinipātadhammo niyato sambodhiparāyaṇo.
root_text.sn55.11:2.3: Idha, bhikkhuniyo, ariyasāvako buddhe aveccappasādena samannāgato hoti—
root_text.sn55.11:2.4: itipi so bhagavā …pe… satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavāti.
root_text.sn55.11:2.7: ariyakantehi sīlehi samannāgato hoti, akhaṇḍehi …pe… samādhisaṁvattanikehi.
root_text.sn55.11:2.8: Imehi kho, bhikkhuniyo, catūhi dhammehi samannāgato ariyasāvako sotāpanno hoti avinipātadhammo niyato sambodhiparāyaṇo”ti.

translation_text.sn55.11:0.3: A Saṅgha of a Thousand Nuns
translation_text.sn55.11:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in the Royal Monastery.
translation_text.sn55.11:1.2: Then a Saṅgha of a thousand nuns went up to the Buddha, bowed, and stood to one side. The Buddha said to them:
translation_text.sn55.11:2.1: “Nuns, a noble disciple who has four things is a stream-enterer, not liable to be reborn in the underworld, destined for awakening.
translation_text.sn55.11:2.3: It’s when a noble disciple has experiential confidence in the Buddha …
translation_text.sn55.11:2.7: And they have the ethical conduct loved by the noble ones … leading to immersion.
translation_text.sn55.11:2.8: A noble disciple who has these four things is a stream-enterer, not liable to be reborn in the underworld, destined for awakening.”
